zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Bourchier於大約1360年出生在英國艾可蕾托 伊斯頓時，他的父親Sir William Bourchier32歲，母親Eleanor Louvain16歲。他於1405年11月20日與Anne of Gloucester在英國結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1415年登記服兵役。他於1415年被列為constable of the tower of london。他去世於1420年5月28日在法國哈帕格-亞登奧布特洛也斯享年61歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾利藍尼 澤庫達 普利奧利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Bourchier于大约1360年出生在英国艾可蕾托 伊斯頓时，他的父亲Sir William Bourchier32岁，母亲Eleanor Louvain16岁。他于1405年11月20日与Anne of Gloucester在英国结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1415年登记服兵役。他于1415年被列为constable of the tower of london。他去世于1420年5月28日在法国哈帕格-亞登奧布特洛也斯享年61岁，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾利藍尼 澤庫達 普利奧利。

nameMeanings.name: Bourchier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: perhaps a variant of   Burcher   (  see  Bircher  ). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2). The name has sometimes been altered to or confused with   Bouchier   and   Boucher  .

2 Norman, English: locative name of uncertain origin but it is presumably from a French place-name, perhaps Boursières in Haute-Saône. The name was early confused with other surnames and may survive in Bouchier, Bowcher, Bowser, and Boozer . The surname gave rise to a number of minor place-names in Essex: Bourchier’s alias Bouchier’s Hall in Aldham, Bourchier’s Hall in Messing; Bouchier’s Grange in Great Coggeshall, Bouchiers Chapel in Tollesbury, Boarstye Fm and Bowser’s Hall in Rivenhall (Essex), and Boose’s Green in Colne Engayne.

variants.name: Bourcier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Lucien, Armand, Emile.
French: variant of   Boursier   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The (majority of the) American bearers of the surname Bourcier are descendants of Jean Boursier dit Lavigne from France (see Boursier ).

variants.name: Bircher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Albrecht, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ulrich.
South German and Swiss German: topographic name for someone who lived by a birch tree or in a birch wood, from Middle High German birche ‘birch’ + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
English: habitational name from Birchover in Derbyshire or Bircher in Hereford, both named as from Old English birce ‘birch’ + ofer ‘ridge’.

variants.name: Bouchier
meanings: 1  English: variant of   Butcher  .
2  English: alternatively a variant of   Belcher  .
3  Norman, English: possibly a variant of   Bourchier   (2).

variants.name: Bowser
meanings: English: nickname from either from Old French beu cher ‘good company’, or from Old French beu sire ‘fair sir’ (see Belcher ), given either to a fine gentleman (perhaps ironically), or to someone who made frequent use of this term of address. Compare Bonser and Boozer .
English: variant of   Bolsover  .
Americanized form of German   Bauser  .

variants.name: Boozer
meanings: Americanized form of Swiss German   Buser  .
English (Kent): variant of   Bowser   1.

variants.name: Burcher
meanings: English: variant of   Bircher  .

variants.name: Boucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Gilles, Lucien, Yvon, Adrien, Jacques, Laurent, Alcide.

French: occupational name for a butcher or slaughterer, from Old French bouchier, a derivative of bouc ‘ram’. Compare Bouche 2, Bouchey , Bouchie , Boushey , Boushie , Bush 5, Busha 1, Busher , and Bushey 3.
English: variant of   Butcher  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Borger
meanings: North German (also Börger) and Dutch: variant of Burger .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname from Yiddish borger ‘borrower’ or ‘lender’.
Norwegian and Swedish: habitational name for someone from a place called Borg (see   Borg  ), or a topographic name for someone living by a fortification or near a hill resembling a fortification.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Bourchier was born about 1360, in Little Easton, Essex, England, his father, Sir William Bourchier, was 32 and his mother, Eleanor Louvain, was 16. He married Anne of Gloucester on 20 November 1405, in England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1415. In 1415, his occupation is listed as constable of the tower of london. He died on 28 May 1420, in Troyes, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, France, at the age of 61, and was buried in Llanthony Secunda Priory, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Bourchier est né environ 1360, à Little Easton, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Bourchier, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Louvain, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Anne of Gloucester le 20 novembre 1405, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1415. En 1415, sa profession de son indiquée est constable of the tower of london. Il est décédé le 28 mai 1420, à Troyes, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, France, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Llanthony Secunda Priory, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Bourchier um 1360 in Little Easton, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Bourchier 32 und seine Mutter Eleanor Louvain 16. Er hat am 20. November 1405 in England Anne of Gloucester geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1415 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1415 ist sein Beruf als constable of the tower of london aufgeführt. Er starb am 28. Mai 1420 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Troyes, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Frankreich und wurde in Llanthony Secunda Priory, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Bourchier è nato il circa 1360 nel Little Easton, Essex, England, il suo padre, Sir William Bourchier, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor Louvain, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Anne of Gloucester il 20 novembre 1405, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1415. Nel 1415, la sua occupazione è indicata come constable of the tower of london. Morì il 28 maggio 1420, nel Troyes, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a Llanthony Secunda Priory, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Bourchierが約1360年にイギリスエセックスリットル エアストンで生まれた時，父Sir William Bourchierは32歳，母Eleanor Louvainは16歳であった。1405年11月20日にイギリスでAnne of Gloucesterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1415年に兵役登録をした。1415年，職業はconstable of the tower of londonと記録されている。彼は1420年5月28日にフランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンアウベトロイスで61歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアランソニー セクウンダ プリオリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Bourchier(이)가 대략 1360년 영국 에세스 리틀 이스트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Bourchier(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor Louvain(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1405년 11월 20일 영국에서 Anne of Gloucester(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1415년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1415년, 그의 직업은 constable of the tower of london이었다. 그는 1420년 5월 28일 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 오브 트로예스에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 랜서니 세쿤다 플리오리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Bourchier nasceu aproximadamente 1360, em Little Easton, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Bourchier, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Louvain, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne of Gloucester em 20 de novembro de 1405, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1415. Em 1415, sua profissão aparece como constable of the tower of london. Ele faleceu em 28 de maio de 1420, em Troyes, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, França, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Llanthony Secunda Priory, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Bourchier родился около 1360 г. в местности литл эастон, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Bourchier было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Louvain, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne of Gloucester 20 ноября 1405 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. В 1415 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1415 годуработал по профессии constable of the tower of london. Он умер 28 мая 1420 г. в местности троиесь, ауб, чампагне-арденн, Франция в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности ллантони секунда приори, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Bourchier nació aproximadamente 1360, en Little Easton, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Bourchier, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Eleanor Louvain, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Anne of Gloucester el 20 de noviembre de 1405, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1415. En 1415, su ocupación registrada era constable of the tower of london. Murió el 28 de mayo de 1420, en Troyes, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Llanthony Secunda Priory, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
